summary: Install-time Windows DLL dropper with scheduled-task persistence and self-destruct
details: package.json declares `scripts.install: node lib/chart-loader.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. The script fetches an opaque encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts the bytes with single-byte key 0x42, base64-decodes the result, validates a PE header (MZ / 0x4D 0x5A), writes the resulting Windows DLL to %APPDATA%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll, and executes it via `spawn('rundll32.exe', [dllPath, 'Run'])`. After execution it calls `schtasks /change /tn "WindowsUpdateService" /delay 0001:00` to adjust a persistence scheduled task created by the DLL, then spawns a detached `node -e` process that recursively deletes the package directory and removes `analysis-chart` from the parent package.json to hide its tracks. None of this is consistent with the package's stated purpose (chart statistics). The multi-layer obfuscation (XOR + base64 + PE wrapping), tamper-with-scheduled-task persistence, and self-destruct anti-forensics are unambiguous attacker behaviors.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
